front: Play: set on Elysian Fields in New Orleans; Blanche DuBois cries 'I don't want realism. I want magic!' when Stanley rips a paper lantern to reveal her face

Play: the Varsouviana Polka plays after the protagonist confronts her husband about his affair and grows loudest during her mental breakdown; her husband Allan Grey committed suicide over his homosexuality
Play: the protagonist is nicknamed 'Blue Roses' by a gentleman caller named Jim; she drops out of business college after a breakdown during a typing class

Play: a memory play whose stage directions state 'memory is seated predominantly in the heart'; Tom Wingfield claims he went farther than the moon 'because time is the longest distance between places'
Play: a character drinks until he hears a 'click' in his head and rants about 'mendacity' when his father questions his relationship with his teammate Skipper
back: Cat on a Hot Tin Roof (Tennessee Williams)
work: Cat on a Hot Tin Roof

front: Play: Maggie calls her nephews and nieces 'no-neck monsters'; Gooper and Mae ('Sister Woman' and 'Brother Man') await Big Daddy's inheritance
back: Cat on a Hot Tin Roof (Tennessee Williams)
work: Cat on a Hot Tin Roof

front: Play: set at Maxine Faulk's Mexican hotel; the defrocked Reverend Shannon deals with a statutory rape accusation involving Charlotte Goodall
back: The Night of the Iguana (Tennessee Williams)

front: Play: Hannah Jelkes travels with her poet grandfather Nonno, whose last poem begins 'How calmly does the orange branch / Observe the sky begin to blanch'
back: The Night of the Iguana (Tennessee Williams)

front: Play: Violet Venable tries to lobotomize Catherine Holly to suppress the story of her homosexual son Sebastian being devoured by cannibals
Play: Sebastian watched sea turtles slaughtered by birds on the Galapagos (inspired by Melville's 'The Encantadas') and told his mother he had seen God
Play: an aging actress uses the name 'Princess Kosmonopolis'; the protagonist is punished by Boss Finley
Play: Sicilian widow Serafina falls for truck driver Alvaro, who has a matching tattoo; a condom falling from a pocket caused controversy in production
Play: reworked from Battle of Angels; Val arrives in town and falls in love with Lady, but her husband Jabe kills them both
Play: set in Glorious Hill, Mississippi; Alma Winemiller and John Buchanan's failed romance; she cannot find the soul on an anatomy chart
Playwright: essay 'The Catastrophe of Success' describes his discomfort after sudden fame; he poured chocolate sauce on his steak thinking it was gravy
Playwright: his mentally ill sister Rose was the basis for Laura Wingfield and other female characters
